title: SNAP, MEDICAID, AND TRANSPORTATION SERVICES - 211info
text: EAST AREA OFFICE SNAP, MEDICAID, AND TRANSPORTATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services to older adults, adults with disabilities, veterans, and caregivers. Services include Medicaid case management, SNAP/food stamps administration, information and assistance, transportation, in-home services subsidies so client can remain independent, advocacy assistance, and help with health care questions. Adult protective services investigates incidents of abuse or neglect involving older adults and people with disabilities who are living in a facility or their own homes and provides a phone line to report abuse or neglect. Orientation to become a home care worker (HCW) is held monthly at this location. 600 NE 8th Street Suite 100 Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Age 60 and older, adults with disabilities, veterans, and caregivers Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 988-3840 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 988-3840 Intake (503) 988-0351 TTY/TTD (503) 988-5678 HCW Unit (503) 988-7575 ext 4 Adult Protective Services (503) 988-4450 Reporting Line Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information "Open Enrollment" HCWs (individuals who do not already have a client that they plan to work for) can sign up for orientation each month starting at 8am on the first business day of the month. Call the main number (ask for Homecare Worker Unit) or call HCW unit directly. FEES: None for most programs DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Multnomah County on or east of 162nd Avenue (parts of zip codes 97014, 97230, 97233, and 97236 and all of zip codes 97019, 97024, 97030, 97060, and 97080) TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Across the street from the Gresham Transit Center Services: Case/Care Management * Medicaid Recipients Food Stamps/SNAP * Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Food Stamps/SNAP * Older Adults Food Stamps/SNAP * Veterans Medicaid * Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Medicaid * Older Adults Medicaid * Veterans Non-Emergency Medical Transportation * Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Non-Emergency Medical Transportation * Older Adults Non-Emergency Medical Transportation * Veterans Other Services Offered At This Location: HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - SENIOR HEALTH INSURANCE BENEFITS ASSISTANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. EAST AREA OFFICE
